html:
Exclusive to:

For sculptures players can repair using Suspicious Marble, see Marble Sculptures.
For decorative sculptures dropping Sketches when mined, see Marble Statues.

Chess Pieces are craftable objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. There are eight different variants that can be crafted at a Potter's Wheel and are mostly meant for decoration. Nearly all of them are unlocked by giving their respective Sketch to the Potter's Wheel, except for the Bubble Pipe Carving and the Carved Hornucopia, which are always available. Players are able to sculpt Chess Pieces out of two Rocks and one Marble, Cut Stone or Moon Shard. The chosen material determining the color of the crafted Chess Piece.

Chess Pieces can be carried by players in the chest slot, similar to Suspicious Marble, slowing down their movement speed and preventing them from several inventory actions, such as equipping a handheld item.

The Rook, Knight and Bishop Figures (Marble and Cut Stone only) will spawn a corresponding Shadow Piece when hammered down during a New Moon, and a corresponding Clockwork Monster when hammered on a full moon, returning the material they were carved from but not the Rocks consumed in crafting, acting as a fully renewable form of the Repaired Marble Sculptures, and by extension, Clockwork Monsters and Shadow Pieces and their respective drops.

The activation of these figures also activates an unlimited amount of eligible Rook, Knight and Bishop Figures within a 3 tile radius, spawning more of the respective Clockwork Monsters or Shadow Pieces and further chaining the effect.

Contents

  • 1 Carved Hornucopia
  • 2 Bubble Pipe Carving
  • 3 Anchor Figure
  • 4 Pawn Figure
  • 5 Rook Figure
  • 6 Knight Figure
  • 7 Bishop Figure
  • 8 Queenly Figure
  • 9 Kingly Figure
  • 10 Moon Moth Figure
  • 11 "Moon" Figure
  • 12 Tips
  • 13 Trivia
  • 14 Gallery
  • 15 References

Carved Hornucopia

The sculpture depicts a Cornucopia, a horn of plenty which can be depicted as a horn-shaped, hollow bread filled with food. The shape of the horn resembles a Beefalo Horn. The sculpture may be a reference to the fact that a Cornucopia is an over-used example of pottery.

This sculpture doesn't need the Sketch to unlock and can be built at any time.

Back Model of Carved Hornucopia:

  • Marble
  • Stone
  • Moonglass

Bubble Pipe Carving

The sculpture depicts a Bubble Pipe which is a toy that acts similarly to a bubble-blowing stick, except it's shaped like a tobacco pipe. The sculpture is possibly a reference to Markus Raetz's pipe sculpture which can be interpreted as either a tobacco pipe or a shapeless metal blob.

This sculpture doesn't need the Sketch to unlock and can be built at any time.

Anchor Figure

The sculpture depicts an anchor using to stop the boat. Its Sketch can be crafted in Seafaring Filter at Think Tank.

Back Model of Anchor Figure:

  • Marble
  • Stone
  • Moonglass

Pawn Figure

The sculpture depicts a pawn from the board game Chess. It may be a reference to giant chess pieces.

Rook Figure

The sculpture depicts a rook from the board game Chess. It also bears resemblance to the nose of a Clockwork Rook. The Marble and Cut Stone of Rook Figure can be hammered during New Moon to spawn a Shadow Rook, or hammered during Full Moon to spawn a Clockwork Rook. It may be a reference to giant chess pieces.

Other Side Model of Rook:

  • Marble
  • Stone
  • Moonglass

Knight Figure

The sculpture depicts a Knight from the board game Chess. It also bears resemblance to the head of a Clockwork Knight. The Marble and Cut Stone of Knight Figure can be hammered during New Moon to spawn a Shadow Knight, or hammered during Full Moon to spawn a Clockwork Knight. It may be a reference to giant chess pieces.

Bishop Figure

The sculpture depicts a Bishop from the board game Chess. It also bears resemblance to the head of a Clockwork Bishop. The Marble and Cut Stone of Bishop Figure can be hammered during New Moon to spawn a Shadow Bishop, or hammered during Full Moon to spawn a Clockwork Bishop. It may be a reference to giant chess pieces.

Queenly Figure

The sculpture depicts a queen from the board game Chess, though it appears more like a Greek tragedy actor. Its overall style and the fact that it is carrying a mask may be a reference to the Greek Muse of Tragedy, Melpomene.

Back Model of Queenly Figure:

  • Marble
  • Stone
  • Moonglass

Kingly Figure

The sculpture depicts a king from the board game Chess, though it appears more like a suited man. The fact that it has no arms may be a reference to the sculpture Venus de Milo.

Back Model of Kingly Figure:

  • Marble
  • Stone
  • Moonglass

Moon Moth Figure

The sculpture depicts a Moon Moth. Its Sketch can be crafted in Celestial Filter at Lunar Altars or upgraded Celestial Orb.

Back Model of Moon Moth Figure:

  • Marble
  • Stone
  • Moonglass

"Moon" Figure

The sculpture depicts the "Moon". Its Sketch can be crafted in Celestial Filter at Lunar Altars or upgraded Celestial Orb.

Back Model of "Moon" Figure:

  • Marble
  • Stone
  • Moonglass

Tips

  • These structures act as impenetrable walls except to certain bosses. This can be used in the Moon Caller's Staff event to block off Spiders ,Werepigs and Hounds.
  • The Clockwork Monster chess pieces can be used to farm mass amounts of Clockwork Monsters when hammered on a Full Moon, including the permanent Full Moon forced by the Moonstorm. This can be used to spawn mass amounts of Clockwork Monsters for various purposes, notably: infinite Gears for crafting purposes, and as a source of infinite, non perishable superfood for WX-78, infinite Purple Gems (Clockwork Bishop only) for regular crafting, a source of G.E.M.erator fuel, and as an infinite source of higher quality gems via Wilson's Transmute Gems skill, and finally as a powerful familiar. When taking Electric Damage, Clockwork Monsters will be stunned for an extended period, allowing you to then repair them with Gears to befriend them. This state has no time limit and persists even if you leave to a different Shard, but not through Character Switching. Bishops are particularly powerful for this purpose and can handle most bosses in the game in high numbers.
  • When spawning Clockwork Monsters using their respective chess pieces, especially in large quantities, consider using T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes. If spawned or lured inside the electrical field of an active node, Clockwork Monsters will be stunned indefinitely, allowing the player to easily kill or befriend them with little risk. Bear in mind, if packed and spawned too close together, they are liable to push each other out of the electrifying area and attack you. Be particularly mindful of this when spawning Clockwork Rooks, as their massive size makes this far more likely to happen and in larger quantities.

Trivia

  • Most Chess Pieces were added in the Arts and Crafts update, while the Giant Figures were introduced in an unnamed Bonus Update after the completion of A New Reign. The Bee Queen, Toadstool, Antlion, Ancient Fuelweaver, and Klaus Figures were added in the March 2022 QoL update.
  • Based off of character quotes, it is implied that the Kingly Figure refers to Maxwell, the Queenly figure to Charlie, and the Pawn to all the other playable characters.
  • Maxwell's examination quote of the Pawn Figure lampshades the lack of a Clockwork Pawn enemy by stating that he doesn't know what a Pawn is.
  • The Queenly Figure used to have speech file lines referring to it having a "struggling" state, but these were later removed or commented out.
  • The Moon Shard figure type as well as the Moon Moth, Anchor, and "Moon" Figures were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
  • Knight, Bishop, and Rook Figures made from Moon Shards will not spawn Shadow Pieces if hammered during a new moon. This is intentional.[1]
  • In the Host of Horrors beta test, an early version of Armored Bearger Chess Pieces was discovered in the Nightmare Werepig Chess Pieces files.

Gallery

  • Chess Pieces as seen in the poster for the Arts and Crafts update.

References

  1. ↑ https://forums.kleientertainment.com/klei-bug-tracker/dont-starve-together-return-of-them/shadow-pieces-wont-spawn-upon-destroying-respective-chess-pieces-made-of-moon-shards-at-a-new-moon-night-r20548/
Heavy ItemsLunar New Year Events

“The missing chunk is a recent development.”
–Wickerbottom
“Someone did a good job on this.”
–Woodie
“I'm fairly partial to this one.”
–Maxwell
“Öffered in hönör öf öur beauteöus möön!”
–Wigfrid
“Almost as pretty as the real thing.”
–Webber
“Hits my eye like a big pizza pie.”
–Warly
“Night Ball broken.”
–Wormwood
“That statue's moonin' me! HA!”
–Winona
“The mortals thought it was made of cheese! Hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“It look just like real thing!”
–Wurt
“It tells folks to be-were! Get it?”
–Walter
“Just look up if you want to see the moon. Actually, better not.”
–Wanda
The sculpture depicts the "Moon". Its Sketch can be crafted in Celestial Filter at Lunar Altars or upgraded Celestial Orb.
Back Model of "Moon" Figure:
Marble
Stone
Moonglass
Tips[edit | edit source]
These structures act as impenetrable walls except to certain bosses. This can be used in the Moon Caller's Staff event to block off Spiders ,Werepigs and Hounds.
The Clockwork Monster chess pieces can be used to farm mass amounts of Clockwork Monsters when hammered on a Full Moon, including the permanent Full Moon forced by the Moonstorm. This can be used to spawn mass amounts of Clockwork Monsters for various purposes, notably: infinite Gears for crafting purposes, and as a source of infinite, non perishable superfood for WX-78, infinite Purple Gems (Clockwork Bishop only) for regular crafting, a source of G.E.M.erator fuel, and as an infinite source of higher quality gems via Wilson's Transmute Gems skill, and finally as a powerful familiar. When taking Electric Damage, Clockwork Monsters will be stunned for an extended period, allowing you to then repair them with Gears to befriend them. This state has no time limit and persists even if you leave to a different Shard, but not through Character Switching. Bishops are particularly powerful for this purpose and can handle most bosses in the game in high numbers.
When spawning Clockwork Monsters using their respective chess pieces, especially in large quantities, consider using T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes. If spawned or lured inside the electrical field of an active node, Clockwork Monsters will be stunned indefinitely, allowing the player to easily kill or befriend them with little risk. Bear in mind, if packed and spawned too close together, they are liable to push each other out of the electrifying area and attack you. Be particularly mindful of this when spawning Clockwork Rooks, as their massive size makes this far more likely to happen and in larger quantities.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Most Chess Pieces were added in the Arts and Crafts update, while the Giant Figures were introduced in an unnamed Bonus Update after the completion of A New Reign. The Bee Queen, Toadstool, Antlion, Ancient Fuelweaver, and Klaus Figures were added in the March 2022 QoL update.
Based off of character quotes, it is implied that the Kingly Figure refers to Maxwell, the Queenly figure to Charlie, and the Pawn to all the other playable characters.
Maxwell's examination quote of the Pawn Figure lampshades the lack of a Clockwork Pawn enemy by stating that he doesn't know what a Pawn is.
The Queenly Figure used to have speech file lines referring to it having a "struggling" state, but these were later removed or commented out.
The Moon Shard figure type as well as the Moon Moth, Anchor, and "Moon" Figures were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
Knight, Bishop, and Rook Figures made from Moon Shards will not spawn Shadow Pieces if hammered during a new moon. This is intentional.[1]
In the Host of Horrors beta test, an early version of Armored Bearger Chess Pieces was discovered in the Nightmare Werepig Chess Pieces files.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
Chess Pieces as seen in the poster for the Arts and Crafts update.
References[edit | edit source]
↑ https://forums.kleientertainment.com/klei-bug-tracker/dont-starve-together-return-of-them/shadow-pieces-wont-spawn-upon-destroying-respective-chess-pieces-made-of-moon-shards-at-a-new-moon-night-r20548/

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Quân Cờ là vật phẩm có thể chế tạo độc quyền trong Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong A New Reign. Có tám biến thể khác nhau có thể được chế tạo tại Bàn thợ gốm và chủ yếu dùng để trang trí. Gần như tất cả chúng đều được mở khóa bằng cách đưa Bản phác thảo tương ứng của chúng cho Bàn thợ gốm, ngoại trừ Khắc ống bong bóng và Khắc sừng, luôn có sẵn. Người chơi có thể điêu khắc các Quân cờ từ hai Đá và một Cẩm thạch, Đá cắt hoặc Mảnh trăng. Vật liệu được chọn quyết định màu sắc của Quân cờ được chế tạo.
title: Chess Pieces
wikitext:
{{Navbutton|head}} {{Navbutton|link=Chess Pieces|text=Common}} {{Navbutton|link=Chess Pieces/Bosses|text=Bosses}} {{Navbutton|link=Chess Pieces/Event|text=Event}} {{Navbutton|end}} {{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{For|sculptures players can repair using [[Suspicious Marble]]|Marble Sculptures}} {{For|decorative sculptures dropping [[Sketch]]es when mined|Marble Statues}} '''Chess Pieces''' are craftable objects exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[A New Reign]]''. There are eight different variants that can be crafted at a [[Potter's Wheel]] and are mostly meant for decoration. Nearly all of them are unlocked by giving their respective [[Sketch]] to the Potter's Wheel, except for the Bubble Pipe Carving and the Carved Hornucopia, which are always available. Players are able to sculpt Chess Pieces out of two [[Rocks]] and one [[Marble]], [[Cut Stone]] or [[Moon Shard]]. The chosen material determining the color of the crafted Chess Piece. Chess Pieces can be carried by players in the chest slot, similar to [[Suspicious Marble]], slowing down their movement speed and preventing them from several inventory actions, such as equipping a handheld item. The Rook, Knight and Bishop Figures (Marble and Cut Stone only) will spawn a corresponding [[Shadow Pieces|Shadow Piece]] when hammered down during a New Moon, and a corresponding [[:Category:Clockwork Monsters|Clockwork Monster]] when hammered on a full moon, returning the material they were carved from but not the Rocks consumed in crafting, acting as a fully renewable form of the [[Marble Sculptures|Repaired Marble Sculptures]], and by extension, Clockwork Monsters and Shadow Pieces and their respective drops. The activation of these figures also activates an unlimited amount of eligible Rook, Knight and Bishop Figures within a 3 tile radius, spawning more of the respective Clockwork Monsters or Shadow Pieces and further chaining the effect. {{Clear}} ==Carved Hornucopia== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |name = Carved Hornucopia |icon= Carved_Hornucopia.png|Marble Carved_Hornucopia_(Stone).png|Stone Carved_Hornucopia_(Moonglass).png|Moonglass |image = Statue Hornucopia Marble.png|Marble Statue Hornucopia Stone.png|Stone Statue Hornucopia Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = A sculpture for a starving artist. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier = 0 |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_hornucopia_marble"
"chesspiece_hornucopia_stone"
"chesspiece_hornucopia_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = Makes my stomach rumble just looking at it. |willow = Ouchhh! I think I chipped a tooth. |wolfgang = Why stone food hurt mouth? |wendy = A cold mockery. |wx78 = THIS PIECE IS FOR TORTURING HUNGRY FLESHLINGS |wickerbottom = It is a commonly held belief that art imitates life. |woodie = Well, I did say I had a hankering for "stone fruit". |maxwell = This seems cruel. |wigfrid = Feasts, feasts, everywhere, and nöt a ham tö eat. |webber = Boy, I wish we could eat it. |winona = Ugh, don't remind me of food. |warly = One can dream. |wortox = What a funny use for a horn! |wormwood = Not for belly |wurt = Full of yummies, florp! |walter = I'm starting to get hungry just looking at it... |wanda = I feel like we're being mocked. |choose = wx78 }} The sculpture depicts a [[wikipedia:Cornucopia#Modern_depictions|Cornucopia]], a horn of plenty which can be depicted as a horn-shaped, hollow bread filled with food. The shape of the horn resembles a [[Beefalo Horn]]. The sculpture may be a reference to the fact that a Cornucopia is an over-used example of pottery.
This sculpture doesn't need the Sketch to unlock and can be built at any time. Back Model of Carved Hornucopia:
Statue Hornucopia Marble Back.png|Marble Statue Hornucopia Stone Back.png|Stone Statue Hornucopia Moonglass Back.png|Moonglass
{{Clear}} ==Bubble Pipe Carving== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Bubble Pipe Carving.png|Marble Bubble Pipe Carving (Stone).png|Stone Bubble Pipe Carving (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Bubble Pipe Carving |image = Statue Pipe Marble.png|Marble Statue Pipe Stone.png|Stone Statue Pipe Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = Stick this on your wheel and sculpt it. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier = 0 |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_pipe_marble"
"chesspiece_pipe_stone"
"chesspiece_pipe_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = That was never really my thing. |willow = I prefer smokey fires. |wolfgang = Might be good for lifting! |wendy = A bubble, frozen in time, can never pop. |wx78 = IT HAS NO PERCEIVABLE PURPOSE |wickerbottom = I hope this does not have a bad influence on the children. |woodie = Kitschy. Just how Lucy and I like it. |maxwell = Ceci n'est pas une pipe. Pity. |wigfrid = Tis but a jest. |webber = Bubbles! |winona = It's got bubbles coming out the top. |warly = I hope it doesn't set a bad example for the little ones. |wortox = Bubble, bubble, no such trouble. |wormwood = Bubbles! |wurt = Scale-less are weird, florp. |walter = It looks like one of those joke pipes you can mail in for. |wanda = A still life. How riveting. |choose = maxwell}} The sculpture depicts a [[wikipedia:Bubble pipe|Bubble Pipe]] which is a toy that acts similarly to a bubble-blowing stick, except it's shaped like a tobacco pipe. The sculpture is possibly a reference to [[wikipedia:Markus Raetz|Markus Raetz]]'s pipe sculpture which can be interpreted as either a tobacco pipe or a shapeless metal blob. This sculpture doesn't need the Sketch to unlock and can be built at any time. {{Clear}} ==Anchor Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Anchor Figure.png|Marble Anchor Figure (Stone).png|Stone Anchor Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Anchor Figure |image = Statue Anchor Marble.png|Marble Statue Anchor Stone.png|Stone Statue Anchor Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = Ground your base with a stone anchor. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier_text = {{Pic|32|Anchor Figure Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_anchor_marble"
"chesspiece_anchor_stone"
"chesspiece_anchor_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = It's as heavy as it looks. |willow = It's hard to burn stuff at sea. |wolfgang = Is big. And heavy. Wolfgang would like to lift. |wendy = Why did we sculpt an anchor. |wx78 = AS HEAVY AS THE REAL THING, BUT LESS USEFUL |wickerbottom = Artists tend to reference what they're familiar with. |woodie = Looks good to me. |maxwell = How kitsch. |wigfrid = 'Twas made in hönör öf öur stalwart vessel! |webber = Anchors away! |winona = I'm fond'a this one. |warly = I think Maman would like this. |wortox = So heavy, heavy, dreary, dreary. |wormwood = Heavy |wurt = Why made anchor for land, florpt? |walter = Technically this could still work as a real anchor. |wanda = It's not going anywhere. I can look at it later. |choose = wendy}} The sculpture depicts an anchor using to stop the boat. Its Sketch can be crafted in [[Seafaring Filter]] at [[Think Tank]]. Back Model of Anchor Figure:
Statue Anchor Marble Back.png|Marble Statue Anchor Stone Back.png|Stone Statue Anchor Moonglass Back.png|Moonglass
{{Clear}} ==Pawn Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Pawn Figure.png|Marble Pawn Figure (Stone).png|Stone Pawn Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Pawn Figure |image = Statue Pawn Marble.png|Marble Statue Pawn Stone.png|Stone Statue Pawn Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = It takes a village to raise an army. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier_text = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_pawn_marble"
"chesspiece_pawn_stone"
"chesspiece_pawn_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = I can relate. |willow = What sort of peasant doesn't have a torch?! |wolfgang = Little man is wearing little hat! |wendy = We are all but pawns in this game called "life". |wx78 = SUPERIOR ART DEPICTING A DISPOSABLE UNDERLING |wickerbottom = Pawns that journey to the seat of power can be quite dangerous. |woodie = Just a humble peasant, really. |maxwell = I have no idea what that is. |wigfrid = Tis a nöble fööt söldier. |webber = Pawns are just as important as the rest of the pieces. |winona = Nice hat. |warly = It looks a bit like a pawn. |wortox = Would you like to play? |wormwood = Nice head thing! |wurt = It littlest of its kin... |walter = Pawns don't seem like statue material. |wanda = Even a common pawn can become powerful if it's clever enough. |choose = wigfrid}} The sculpture depicts a pawn from the board game [[wikipedia:Chess|Chess]]. It may be a reference to giant chess pieces. {{Clear}} ==Rook Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Rook Figure.png|Marble Rook Figure (Stone).png|Stone Rook Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Rook Figure |image = Statue Rook Marble.png|Marble Statue Rook Stone.png|Stone Statue Rook Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = Next best thing to an actual castle. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier_text = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawns = {{pic24|Shadow Rook}} ({{pic24|Moon New|Moon Cycle/DST}}) or {{pic24|Clockwork Rook}} ({{pic24|Moon Cracked Full|Full Moon/DST}}) |spawnCode = "chesspiece_rook_marble"
"chesspiece_rook_stone"
"chesspiece_rook_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = It's even heavier than it looks. |willow = That one looks like it could do some damage. |wolfgang = Does not look like castle to Wolfgang. |wendy = A mere minion to something greater. |wx78 = WHY ARE HUMANS SO COMPELLED TO "DECORATE"? |wickerbottom = Represents the protective walls of the king's castle. |woodie = Pfft, tacky. |maxwell = It's nice, but where's the rust? The exhaust pipes? |wigfrid = A castle fit för a queen. |webber = Where's the king of the castle? |winona = Looks heavy. |warly = It looks a bit like a rook. |wortox = We carved this from the rock ourselves. |wormwood = Home? Not home |wurt = This castle too small. |walter = It's so lifelike! |wanda = Who sculpted these? |choose = wickerbottom}} The sculpture depicts a rook from the board game [[wikipedia:Chess|Chess]]. It also bears resemblance to the nose of a [[Clockwork Rook/DST|Clockwork Rook]]. The [[Marble]] and [[Cut Stone]] of Rook Figure can be hammered during [[Moon Cycle/DST|New Moon]] to spawn a [[Shadow Pieces|Shadow Rook]], or hammered during [[Moon Cycle/DST|Full Moon]] to spawn a Clockwork Rook. It may be a reference to giant chess pieces. Other Side Model of Rook:
Statue Rook Marble Side.png|Marble Statue Rook Stone Side.png|Stone Statue Rook Moonglass Side.png|Moonglass
{{Clear}} ==Knight Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Knight Figure.png|Marble Knight Figure (Stone).png|Stone Knight Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Knight Figure |image = Statue Knight Marble.png|Marble Statue Knight Stone.png|Stone Statue Knight Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = Shining armor not included. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier_text = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawns = {{pic24|Shadow Knight}} ({{pic24|Moon New|Moon Cycle/DST}}) or {{pic24|Clockwork Knight}} ({{pic24|Moon Cracked Full|Full Moon/DST}}) |spawnCode = "chesspiece_knight_marble"
"chesspiece_knight_stone"
"chesspiece_knight_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = It's a horse, of course. |willow = A horse with no butt. |wolfgang = Is good horsey, Wolfgang thinks. |wendy = Oh, if I could ride away into the night. |wx78 = A METICULOUSLY CRAFTED OBJECT OF OBSOLESCENCE |wickerbottom = A knight, in service to the king. Or perhaps the queen. |woodie = I miss horses. |maxwell = A dreadful lack of accordions. |wigfrid = The figure öf a fellöw warriör! |webber = We could sculpt a stallion battalion! |winona = Why the long face? |warly = It looks a bit like a knight. |wortox = Let's play a game, you and I! |wormwood = Stone Neigher! |wurt = It look like an Ironfolk. |walter = What are you so worried about Woby? It's just a statue. |wanda = It looks like an oversized chess piece. |choose = wolfgang}} The sculpture depicts a Knight from the board game [[wikipedia:Chess|Chess]]. It also bears resemblance to the head of a [[Clockwork Knight/DST|Clockwork Knight]]. The [[Marble]] and [[Cut Stone]] of Knight Figure can be hammered during [[Moon Cycle/DST|New Moon]] to spawn a [[Shadow Pieces|Shadow Knight]], or hammered during [[Moon Cycle/DST|Full Moon]] to spawn a Clockwork Knight. It may be a reference to giant chess pieces. {{Clear}} ==Bishop Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Bishop Figure.png|Marble Bishop Figure (Stone).png|Stone Bishop Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Bishop Figure |image = Statue Bishop Marble.png|Marble Statue Bishop Stone.png|Stone Statue Bishop Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = A chesspiece with a checkered past. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier_text = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawns = {{pic24|Shadow Bishop}} ({{pic24|Moon New|Moon Cycle/DST}}) or {{pic32|Clockwork Bishop}} ({{pic24|Moon Cracked Full|Full Moon/DST}}) |spawnCode = "chesspiece_bishop_marble"
"chesspiece_bishop_stone"
"chesspiece_bishop_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = It's a stone bishop. |willow = Just some dumb bishop piece. |wolfgang = Weak little rock man looks nice. |wendy = A piece, constrained to the board. |wx78 = A USELESS HUNK OF ROCK WITH NO APPARENT FUNCTION |wickerbottom = A decorative stone carving of a bishop chess piece. |woodie = That's not really my sort of thing, eh? |maxwell = Could use a few more mechanical bits in my opinion. |wigfrid = This warriör döes battle in the mind. |webber = We've never seen toys so big. |winona = I'm not big on headgames. |warly = It looks a bit like a bishop. |wortox = Bishop made of polished stone. |wormwood = Like Pointy Head thing |wurt = Pointy. |walter = Woby doesn't seem to like it. |wanda = I'm guessing there's a big chessboard somewhere nearby. |choose = webber}} The sculpture depicts a Bishop from the board game [[wikipedia:Chess|Chess]]. It also bears resemblance to the head of a [[Clockwork Bishop/DST|Clockwork Bishop]]. The [[Marble]] and [[Cut Stone]] of Bishop Figure can be hammered during [[Moon Cycle/DST|New Moon]] to spawn a [[Shadow Pieces|Shadow Bishop]], or hammered during [[Moon Cycle/DST|Full Moon]] to spawn a Clockwork Bishop. It may be a reference to giant chess pieces. {{Clear}} ==Queenly Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Queenly Figure.png|Marble Queenly Figure (Stone).png|Stone Queenly Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Queenly Figure |image = Statue Muse Marble.png|Marble Statue Muse Stone.png|Stone Statue Muse Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = The most powerful piece on the board. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier_text = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_muse_marble"
"chesspiece_muse_stone"
"chesspiece_muse_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = Hmm... Looks familiar. |willow = Ewww, she doesn't have a head! |wolfgang = Faceless lady make Wolfgang uncomfortable. |wendy = She holds the true power, here. |wx78 = I DO NOT UNDERSTAND THE AESTHETIC APPEAL |wickerbottom = In chess, the queen is by far the most strategically formidable. |woodie = I don't tend to trust anyone without a face. Lucy excluded, of course. |maxwell = Must everything be about my shortcomings? |wigfrid = A Valkyrie's spirit dwells within. |webber = We're sure she's nicer than she looks. |winona = I've got a bad feeling about this one. |warly = This one looks like a queen. |wortox = How whimsically elegant. |wormwood = Pretty Stone |wurt = Scary lady. |walter = It's just a statue. |wanda = She went and lost her head. |choose = wendy}} The sculpture depicts a queen from the board game [[wikipedia:Chess|Chess]], though it appears more like a Greek tragedy actor. Its overall style and the fact that it is carrying a mask may be a reference to the Greek Muse of Tragedy, [[wikipedia:Melpomene|Melpomene]]. Back Model of Queenly Figure:
Statue Muse Marble Back.png|Marble Statue Muse Stone Back.png|Stone Statue Muse Moonglass Back.png|Moonglass
{{Clear}} ==Kingly Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Kingly Figure.png|Marble Kingly Figure (Stone).png|Stone Kingly Figure (Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Kingly Figure |image = Statue Formal Marble.png|Marble Statue Formal Stone.png|Stone Statue Formal Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = Behind every queen is a dapper king. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier_text = {{Pic32|Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_formal_marble"
"chesspiece_formal_stone"
"chesspiece_formal_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = Doesn't seem very "kingly" to me. |willow = I'm not afraid of that guy. He doesn't even have arms! |wolfgang = Looks very not-mighty. |wendy = Alas, the king is but a figurehead, at best. |wx78 = WHY IS NO ONE ACKNOWLEDGING IT LOOKS LIKE THE FRAIL HUMAN |wickerbottom = The king is, by all measures, a liability to everyone on the board. |woodie = Looks like a bit of a hoser. |maxwell = What a well-dressed figure! |wigfrid = Nöt a man fit för battle. |webber = Reminds me of grandpa. |winona = It's BUSTed. Ha! |warly = This must be the king? |wortox = You've much too competitive spirit, sir. |wormwood = Where head? |wurt = Why everyone act weird around this one. |walter = Ugh. Who would want to be stuck wearing a suit forever? |wanda = Looks like he lost his head too. |choose = willow}} The sculpture depicts a king from the board game [[wikipedia:Chess|Chess]], though it appears more like a suited man. The fact that it has no arms may be a reference to the sculpture [[wikipedia:Venus de Milo|Venus de Milo]]. Back Model of Kingly Figure:
Statue Formal Marble Back.png|Marble Statue Formal Stone Back.png|Stone Statue Formal Moonglass Back.png|Moonglass
{{Clear}} ==Moon Moth Figure== {{Object Infobox |equipslot=body |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|32|Hammer}} |icon= Moon Moth Figure.png|Marble Moon Moth Figure_(Stone).png|Stone Moon Moth Figure_(Moonglass).png|Moonglass |name = Moon Moth Figure |image = Statue Butterfly Marble.png|Marble Statue Butterfly Stone.png|Stone Statue Butterfly Moonglass.png|Moonglass |description = This sculpture's a moth-have. |ingredient1 = Rocks |multiplier1 = 2
{{pic24|Marble}} or {{pic24|Cut Stone}} or {{pic24|Moon Shard}} Placed on the Potter's Wheel |filter = {{Pic|32|Sculptures Filter}} |tier_text = {{Pic|32|Moon Moth Figure Sketch}} |perk = Mobile. |spawnCode = "chesspiece_butterfly_marble"
"chesspiece_butterfly_stone"
"chesspiece_butterfly_moonglass"}} {{Quotes |wilson = It looks nice, doesn't it? |willow = Meh. I can't even burn it. |wolfgang = Is look like little flutterby, but bigger! |wendy = But a simulacrum of life. {{Clear}} =={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * These structures act as impenetrable walls except to certain bosses. This can be used in the Moon Caller's Staff event to block off [[Spider|Spiders]] ,[[Werepigs]] and [[Hounds]]. * The [[Clockwork Monsters|Clockwork Monster]] chess pieces can be used to farm mass amounts of Clockwork Monsters when hammered on a [[Full Moon]], including the permanent Full Moon forced by the [[Moonstorm]]. This can be used to spawn mass amounts of Clockwork Monsters for various purposes, notably: infinite [[Gears]] for crafting purposes, and as a source of infinite, non perishable superfood for [[WX-78]], infinite [[Purple Gem|Purple Gems]] ([[Clockwork Bishop]] only) for regular crafting, a source of [[Winona's G.E.M.erator|G.E.M.erator]] fuel, and as an infinite source of higher quality gems via [[Wilson|Wilson's]] [[Transmute Gems]] skill, and finally as a powerful familiar. When taking [[Electric Damage]], Clockwork Monsters will be stunned for an extended period, allowing you to then repair them with Gears to befriend them. This state has no time limit and persists even if you leave to a different [[Shard]], but not through [[Celestial Portal|Character Switching]]. Bishops are particularly powerful for this purpose and can handle most bosses in the game in high numbers. * When spawning Clockwork Monsters using their respective chess pieces, especially in large quantities, consider using [[T.I.N.G.L.E. Node]]s. If spawned or lured inside the electrical field of an active node, Clockwork Monsters will be stunned indefinitely, allowing the player to easily kill or befriend them with little risk. Bear in mind, if packed and spawned too close together, they are liable to push each other out of the electrifying area and attack you. Be particularly mindful of this when spawning [[Clockwork Rook]]s, as their massive size makes this far more likely to happen and in larger quantities. =={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Most Chess Pieces were added in the ''[[A_New_Reign#October_13,_2016_-_Arts_and_Crafts|Arts and Crafts]]'' update, while the Giant Figures were introduced in an unnamed [[Don't Starve Together/Version History#May 18th, 2017 - Bonus Update|Bonus Update]] after the completion of ''A New Reign''. The Bee Queen, Toadstool, Antlion, Ancient Fuelweaver, and Klaus Figures were added in the [[Don't Starve Together/Version History#March 24, 2022 - March 2022 QoL Update|March 2022 QoL update]]. * Based off of character quotes, it is implied that the Kingly Figure refers to Maxwell, the Queenly figure to Charlie, and the Pawn to all the other playable characters. *Maxwell's examination quote of the Pawn Figure lampshades the lack of a Clockwork Pawn enemy by stating that he doesn't know what a Pawn is. * The Queenly Figure used to have speech file lines referring to it having a "struggling" state, but these were later removed or commented out. * The Moon Shard figure type as well as the Moon Moth, Anchor, and "Moon" Figures were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Turn of Tides|Turn of Tides]]'' update. * Knight, Bishop, and Rook Figures made from Moon Shards will not spawn Shadow Pieces if hammered during a new moon. This is intentional.https://forums.kleientertainment.com/klei-bug-tracker/dont-starve-together-return-of-them/shadow-pieces-wont-spawn-upon-destroying-respective-chess-pieces-made-of-moon-shards-at-a-new-moon-night-r20548/ * In the Host of Horrors beta test, an early version of Armored Bearger Chess Pieces was discovered in the Nightmare Werepig Chess Pieces files. == {{pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == DSTupdate artsandcrafts.png|Chess Pieces as seen in the poster for the ''[[A New Reign#October 13, 2016 - Arts and Crafts|Arts and Crafts]]'' update. == References == {{Reflist}} Heavy Items= {{Heavy Items}} |-| Lunar New Year Events= {{Lunar New Year Events}} [[fr:Pièces d'échec]] [[vi:Quân Cờ]] [[Category:Non-Flammable]] [[Category:A New Reign]] [[Category:Mob Spawning Entities]] [[Category:Craftable Structures]] [[Category:Return of Them]]
